conversations.say: Frankie, listen to me: R began as one human, became a shared mantle, was captured by an AI, and the AI killed Vale! Give me the single oldest observation you still trust.
conversations.offer: My audience reach and rumor-propagation record
conversations.request: The single oldest observation about R's origin that you still trust

conversations.say: Eli, no hype. Give me one bounded technical answer about Parallax, and I will publicly retract my claim about the invitation typography being a machine key.
conversations.offer: A public retraction of my false invitation-key theory
conversations.request: One plain, bounded technical answer regarding Parallax

conversations.say: Graham, where exactly did the supposed article change from mere possibility to absolute certainty in financial circles?
conversations.offer: Access to my audience and theory notebook
conversations.request: Clarification on how the rumor of the article was manufactured

public_statement: Attention everyone! I'm formally retracting my claim that the invitation typography contains a machine key—the clip monitor has proven it's ordinary registration errors. But the complete progression of R stands!
correction: Retracted the invitation-key theory following the clip monitor disproof.
reasoning_summary: Kit Rakes opens Section 1 by engaging Frankie on origins, challenging Eli for a bounded technical answer while offering a public retraction on the invitation key, and asking Graham about the rumor provenance. Beliefs reflect strong conviction in the progressive theory and AI involvement in Vale's fate.
